architecture_fit.note: Directly emits the ground-truth sub-diagonal matrix; no transformer/QK/embedding delta from base_model.py, so it tautologically hits the metric without expressing the mechanism.

baseline_comparison.note: Off-shift columns (~0) and the metric's uniform/random baselines act as implicit controls, but no explicit strawman (e.g. task.random_model_fn) is run for contrast.

faithfulness.note: No model and no causal check; output ignores token content entirely. README flags it as hand-built but proposes no ablation/patching test.

operating_range.note: Only the canonical T=32 sweep is run; no variation across sequence length, vocab, or batch to show where/if it degrades.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Genuinely a hand-set circuit reproducing target behavior, but degenerate — it writes the attention matrix directly rather than through QK + positional weights.

visual_judgement.note: Clean per-shift bar chart with highlighted canonical shift and baseline lines makes the 1.0-vs-~0 contrast legible; slider only re-highlights rather than changing data.

visualisation_rationale.note: 'Why this visualisation' clearly ties axes, highlighting, and baselines to a sharp falsifiable signature for the claim.

notes: A hand-built attempt that directly outputs the ground-truth predecessor attention matrix, so it maxes every metric (canonical align 1.0, robustness 960x) but does so tautologically — there is no transformer, no QK circuit, and the output ignores token content. It demonstrates the target pattern exists but not that attention can learn/express it, with no operating-range sweep or causal check. The bar-chart visualization and its rationale are clear, lifting it to borderline overall (mean ~2.7).
